# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses merge request title and description in build notifications and commit messages. This helps track which features are being deployed and generates automatic release notes. Include the MR title in deployment tags.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - validate
  - build
  - test
  - deploy
  - notify

variables:
  SECURE_LOG_LEVEL: "info"
  MAX_TITLE_LENGTH: "200"
  MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH: "2000"

# Validate and sanitize MR inputs before any downstream usage
validate_mr_metadata:
  stage: validate
  rules:
    - if: $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH
  image: alpine:3.19
  script:
    - apk add --no-cache coreutils
    # Sanitize MR title - strip control characters, shell metacharacters, and enforce length limit
    - |
      sanitize_input() {
        local input="$1"
        local max_length="$2"
        local label="$3"
        # Remove null bytes, control characters (except newline/tab for description), and shell-dangerous characters
        sanitized=$(printf '%s' "$input" | tr -d '\000-\010\013\014\016-\037' | sed 's/[`$\\!;&|<>{}()\x27\x22]//g' | head -c "$max_length")
        if [ -z "$sanitized" ]; then
          echo "WARNING: ${label} is empty after sanitization, using fallback"
          sanitized="No ${label} provided"
        fi
        printf '%s' "$sanitized"
      }
    # Sanitize title
    - SAFE_MR_TITLE=$(sanitize_input "${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TITLE:-No MR title}" "$MAX_TITLE_LENGTH" "MR title")
    - echo "SAFE_MR_TITLE=${SAFE_MR_TITLE}" > mr_metadata.env
    # Sanitize description (allow newlines but convert to spaces for single-line contexts)
    - SAFE_MR_DESC=$(sanitize_input "${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DESCRIPTION:-No description provided}" "$MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH" "MR description")
    - SAFE_MR_DESC_ONELINE=$(printf '%s' "$SAFE_MR_DESC" | tr '\n' ' ' | sed 's/  */ /g' | head -c "$MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH")
    - echo "SAFE_MR_DESC_ONELINE=${SAFE_MR_DESC_ONELINE}" >> mr_metadata.env
    # Store multi-line description safely in a file (not env var) for release notes
    - printf '%s' "$SAFE_MR_DESC" > mr_description_sanitized.txt
    # Sanitize MR IID and source branch
    - |
      SAFE_MR_IID=$(printf '%s' "${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID:-0}" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+$' || echo "0")
      echo "SAFE_MR_IID=${SAFE_MR_IID}" >> mr_metadata.env
    - |
      SAFE_SOURCE_BRANCH=$(printf '%s' "${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_SOURCE_BRANCH_NAME:-unknown}" | sed 's/[^a-zA-Z0-9._\/-]//g' | head -c 255)
      echo "SAFE_SOURCE_BRANCH=${SAFE_SOURCE_BRANCH}" >> mr_metadata.env
    # Validate commit SHA format
    - |
      SAFE_COMMIT_SHA=$(printf '%s' "${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA:-unknown}" | grep -oE '^[a-f0-9]{8}$' || echo "unknown")
      echo "SAFE_COMMIT_SHA=${SAFE_COMMIT_SHA}" >> mr_metadata.env
    # Log sanitized metadata (never log raw unsanitized input)
    - echo "Validated MR metadata for MR !${SAFE_MR_IID}"
    - echo "Sanitized title length - $(printf '%s' "$SAFE_MR_TITLE" | wc -c) bytes"
  artifacts:
    reports:
      dotenv: mr_metadata.env
    paths:
      - mr_description_sanitized.txt
    expire_in: 1 hour

build:
  stage: build
  needs:
    - job: validate_mr_metadata
      artifacts: true
  rules:
    - if: $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH
  image: docker:24
  services:
    - docker:24-dind
  variables:
    DOCKER_TLS_CERTDIR: "/certs"
  script:
    # Use only sanitized variables from the validate stage
    - echo "Building for MR !${SAFE_MR_IID} - ${SAFE_MR_TITLE}"
    # Build with sanitized labels - use OCI standard labels
    - |
      docker build \
        --label "org.opencontainers.image.title=${SAFE_MR_TITLE}" \
        --label "org.opencontainers.image.revision=${CI_COMMIT_SHA}" \
        --label "org.opencontainers.image.source=${CI_PROJECT_URL}" \
        --label "org.opencontainers.image.created=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
        --label "com.gitlab.ci.mr.iid=${SAFE_MR_IID}" \
        -t "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_SHA}" \
        -t "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:mr-${SAFE_MR_IID}" \
        .
    # Login using masked CI variable (CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD is masked by default)
    - echo "${CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD}" | docker login -u "${CI_REGISTRY_USER}" --password-stdin "${CI_REGISTRY}"
    - docker push "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_SHA}"
    - |
      if [ "${SAFE_MR_IID}" != "0" ]; then
        docker push "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:mr-${SAFE_MR_IID}"
      fi

test:
  stage: test
  needs:
    - job: validate_mr_metadata
      artifacts: true
    - job: build
  rules:
    - if: $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH
  image: alpine:3.19
  script:
    - echo "Running tests for MR !${SAFE_MR_IID} - ${SAFE_MR_TITLE}"
    - echo "Tests passed"

# Generate release notes from sanitized MR metadata
generate_release_notes:
  stage: deploy
  needs:
    - job: validate_mr_metadata
      artifacts: true
    - job: test
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH
  image: alpine:3.19
  script:
    - apk add --no-cache coreutils
    - DEPLOY_DATE=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    - DEPLOY_TAG="deploy-${SAFE_COMMIT_SHA}-$(date -u +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
    # Validate deploy tag format
    - |
      if ! printf '%s' "$DEPLOY_TAG" | grep -qE '^deploy-[a-f0-9]+-[0-9]+$'; then
        echo "ERROR: Invalid deploy tag format"
        exit 1
      fi
    # Generate release notes file with sanitized content only
    - |
      cat > release_notes.md <<RELEASE_NOTES_EOF
      ## Deployment ${DEPLOY_TAG}

      **Date:** ${DEPLOY_DATE}
      **Commit:** ${SAFE_COMMIT_SHA}
      **MR:** !${SAFE_MR_IID}
      **Branch:** ${SAFE_SOURCE_BRANCH}

      ### Changes

      **${SAFE_MR_TITLE}**

      $(cat mr_description_sanitized.txt)

      ---
      *Auto-generated by CI/CD pipeline ${CI_PIPELINE_ID}*
      RELEASE_NOTES_EOF
    - echo "DEPLOY_TAG=${DEPLOY_TAG}" > deploy_metadata.env
    - echo "DEPLOY_DATE=${DEPLOY_DATE}" >> deploy_metadata.env
    - cat release_notes.md
  artifacts:
    reports:
      dotenv: deploy_metadata.env
    paths:
      - release_notes.md
    expire_in: 30 days

# Create a Git tag for the deployment using the GitLab API
create_deployment_tag:
  stage: deploy
  needs:
    - job: validate_mr_metadata
      artifacts: true
    - job: generate_release_notes
      artifacts: true
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH
  image: alpine:3.19
  script:
    - apk add --no-cache curl jq
    # Validate required variables
    - |
      if [ -z "$DEPLOY_TAG" ] || [ -z "$SAFE_MR_TITLE" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: Missing required deployment metadata"
        exit 1
      fi
    # Create tag message with sanitized content - use jq to safely construct JSON
    # This prevents JSON injection from any residual special characters
    - |
      TAG_MESSAGE=$(printf 'Deploy: %s (MR !%s)' "$SAFE_MR_TITLE" "$SAFE_MR_IID")
    - |
      JSON_PAYLOAD=$(jq -n \
        --arg tag_name "$DEPLOY_TAG" \
        --arg ref "$CI_COMMIT_SHA" \
        --arg message "$TAG_MESSAGE" \
        '{tag_name: $tag_name, ref: $ref, message: $message}')
    # Use CI_JOB_TOKEN with minimum required permissions (api scope not needed, read_api + write_repository)
    # The PROJECT_DEPLOY_TOKEN should be a project/group deploy token with write_repository scope
    # stored as a masked and protected CI/CD variable
    - |
      RESPONSE=$(curl --silent --show-error --fail-with-body \
        --header "PRIVATE-TOKEN: ${PROJECT_DEPLOY_TOKEN}" \
        --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
        --data "$JSON_PAYLOAD" \
        "${CI_API_V4_URL}/projects/${CI_PROJECT_ID}/repository/tags" 2>&1) || {
          # Redact any tokens that might appear in error messages
          SAFE_RESPONSE=$(printf '%s' "$RESPONSE" | sed "s/${PROJECT_DEPLOY_TOKEN:-NOTOKEN}/[REDACTED]/g")
          echo "ERROR: Failed to create tag. Response: ${SAFE_RESPONSE}"
          exit 1
        }
    - echo "Successfully created deployment tag ${DEPLOY_TAG}"
    # Log only safe fields from response
    - printf '%s' "$RESPONSE" | jq '{name: .name, target: .target, message: .message}'

# Create GitLab Release with release notes
create_release:
  stage: deploy
  needs:
    - job: validate_mr_metadata
      artifacts: true
    - job: generate_release_notes
      artifacts: true
    - job: create_deployment_tag
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH
  image: registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-org/release-cli:latest
  script:
    - echo "Creating release for ${DEPLOY_TAG}"
  release:
    tag_name: ${DEPLOY_TAG}
    name: "Release ${DEPLOY_TAG}"
    description: release_notes.md

# Deploy to environment
deploy_production:
  stage: deploy
  needs:
    - job: validate_mr_metadata
      artifacts: true
    - job: generate_release_notes
      artifacts: true
    - job: create_deployment_tag
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH
      when: manual
  image: alpine:3.19
  environment:
    name: production
    url: https://app.example.com
  script:
    - echo "Deploying ${DEPLOY_TAG} to production"
    - echo "MR !${SAFE_MR_IID} - ${SAFE_MR_TITLE}"
    # Actual deployment commands would go here
    # e.g., kubectl set image deployment/app app=${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_SHA}

# Send deployment notification via webhook
notify_deployment:
  stage: notify
  needs:
    - job: validate_mr_metadata
      artifacts: true
    - job: generate_release_notes
      artifacts: true
    - job: deploy_production
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH
  image: alpine:3.19
  script:
    - apk add --no-cache curl jq
    # Build notification payload using jq to prevent injection
    # SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL must be stored as a masked and protected CI/CD variable
    - |
      if [ -z "${SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL}" ]; then
        echo "WARNING: SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL not configured, skipping notification"
        exit 0
      fi
    - |
      NOTIFICATION_PAYLOAD=$(jq -n \
        --arg title "$SAFE_MR_TITLE" \
        --arg mr_iid "$SAFE_MR_IID" \
        --arg tag "$DEPLOY_TAG" \
        --arg date "$DEPLOY_DATE" \
        --arg commit "$SAFE_COMMIT_SHA" \
        --arg branch "$SAFE_SOURCE_BRANCH" \
        --arg project_url "$CI_PROJECT_URL" \
        --arg pipeline_url "$CI_PIPELINE_URL" \
        '{
          text: ("🚀 *Deployment Notification*\n*MR:* !" + $mr_iid + " - " + $title + "\n*Tag:* `" + $tag + "`\n*Commit:* `" + $commit + "`\n*Branch:* " + $branch + "\n*Date:* " + $date + "\n*Pipeline:* " + $pipeline_url),
          unfurl_links: false,
          unfurl_media: false
        }')
    - |
      curl --silent --show-error --fail \
        --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
        --data "$NOTIFICATION_PAYLOAD" \
        --max-time 30 \
        --retry 3 \
        --retry-delay 5 \
        "${SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL}" || {
          echo "WARNING: Failed to send Slack notification"
          # Don't fail the pipeline for notification failures
          exit 0
        }
    - echo "Deployment notification sent successfully"

# Notify on failure
notify_failure:
  stage: notify
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH
      when: on_failure
  image: alpine:3.19
  script:
    - apk add --no-cache curl jq
    - |
      if [ -z "${SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL}" ]; then
        exit 0
      fi
    # Use only CI-provided variables (already trusted) for failure notification
    - |
      FAILURE_PAYLOAD=$(jq -n \
        --arg pipeline_url "$CI_PIPELINE_URL" \
        --arg project "$CI_PROJECT_NAME" \
        --arg commit "$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA" \
        '{
          text: ("❌ *Pipeline Failed*\n*Project:* " + $project + "\n*Commit:* `" + $commit + "`\n*Pipeline:* " + $pipeline_url)
        }')
    - |
      curl --silent --show-error \
        --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
        --data "$FAILURE_PAYLOAD" \
        --max-time 30 \
        "${SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL}" || true
